# docsxai

> Deterministic screenshot docs for web apps. An LLM-agnostic engine plus a Claude Code plugin that walks a running web app, follows written flows, and emits screenshot-rich user documentation. Calibration is AI-assisted and rare; execution is deterministic, agent-free, and CI-friendly.

docsxai treats screenshot docs as a build artifact: a written flow-file is the source, the rendered doc pack is the output, and a re-run is the refresh. The engine ships the `site-docs` CLI, a deterministic Playwright-backed runtime, calibration helpers, and target-site auth strategies; sibling packages add the Claude Code plugin, a standalone MCP server, an authenticated doc-pack backend, a viewer/renderer, and publisher plugins for Starlight and Confluence.

## Start here

- [Introduction](https://docsxai.com/getting-started/introduction/): what docsxai is and the doc loop it implements
- [Installation](https://docsxai.com/getting-started/installation/): install the engine CLI and the Claude Code plugin
- [Quickstart](https://docsxai.com/getting-started/quickstart/): init a workspace, write a flow, run it, render the pack

## Concepts

- [Architecture](https://docsxai.com/concepts/architecture/): how engine, plugin, MCP server, backend, and viewer fit together
- [The doc pack](https://docsxai.com/concepts/doc-pack/): the workspace artifact set - flows, screenshots, annotations, style
- [The browxai ecosystem](https://docsxai.com/concepts/browxai-ecosystem/): live-page discovery during calibration and the sibling tools

## Guides

- [Agent runbook](https://docsxai.com/guides/agent-runbook/): hand a coding agent everything it needs to run site-docs against an app repo
- [Running against an app repo](https://docsxai.com/guides/running-against-an-app-repo/): document an app without leaving a trace in its checkout
- [CI recipes](https://docsxai.com/guides/ci-recipes/): deterministic doc refresh in your pipeline
- [Writing plugins](https://docsxai.com/guides/writing-plugins/): publishers, renderers, lint-rules, and auth-strategy extension points
- [Security best practices](https://docsxai.com/guides/security-best-practices/): operational hardening for adopters
- [Troubleshooting](https://docsxai.com/guides/troubleshooting/): halts, diagnose, lint, and the iteration loop

## Reference

- [CLI](https://docsxai.com/reference/cli/): every site-docs command
- [Flow-file format](https://docsxai.com/reference/flow-file/): the YAML flow schema - steps, locators, waits, annotations, environment
- [Auth strategies](https://docsxai.com/reference/auth-strategies/): manual capture and the scripted strategies
- [Plugins](https://docsxai.com/reference/plugins/): the manifest, the lockfile, and capability declarations
- [MCP tools](https://docsxai.com/reference/mcp-tools/): the docsxai-mcp tool surface
- [Backend API](https://docsxai.com/reference/backend-api/): REST endpoints, OAuth 2.1, doc-pack persistence
- [Actionability contract](https://docsxai.com/reference/actionability/): the portable element-state vocabulary shared with browxai

## Packages

- [@kalebtec/docsxai-engine](https://docsxai.com/packages/engine/): flow-file parser + deterministic runtime + the site-docs CLI
- [@kalebtec/docsxai-plugin](https://docsxai.com/packages/plugin/): the Claude Code plugin
- [@kalebtec/docsxai-mcp](https://docsxai.com/packages/mcp/): the standalone stdio MCP server
- [@kalebtec/docsxai-backend](https://docsxai.com/packages/backend/): the authenticated doc-pack service
- [@kalebtec/docsxai-viewer](https://docsxai.com/packages/viewer/): interactive viewer + burned-annotation renderer
- [@kalebtec/docsxai-skill](https://docsxai.com/packages/skill/): the vendorable skill fallback
- [@kalebtec/docsxai-plugin-confluence](https://docsxai.com/packages/plugin-confluence/): the Confluence publisher plugin
- [@kalebtec/docsxai-plugin-starlight](https://docsxai.com/packages/plugin-starlight/): the Starlight site renderer plugin

## Project

- [Changelog](https://docsxai.com/project/changelog/): all notable changes
- [Contributing](https://docsxai.com/project/contributing/): how to contribute
- [Security](https://docsxai.com/project/security/): the security policy and how to report

## Optional

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/kalebteccom/docsxai): source, issues, and releases
